---
title: "Final Report Rubric"
subtitle: "STAT 184 — Summer 2026 Project"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
  pdf: default
editor: source
---

# Submission

- One PDF per team to Canvas, rendered from a Quarto document in your team's GitHub repo.
- A URL to your 5–7 minute team video presentation.
- Final commit pushed to GitHub repo by the deadline.

**Due:** Friday, June 26, 11:59 p.m.

# Rubric (100 points)

## Report content (60)

| Component | Points |
|---|---|
| Clear introduction and motivation | 5 |
| Research question(s) explicitly stated | 5 |
| Data provenance fully documented | 5 |
| Variables described and justified | 5 |
| Data wrangling explained in narrative (not just code) | 5 |
| Summary table (non-trivial) | 5 |
| Figure 1 with caption, reference, and discussion | 5 |
| Figure 2 with caption, reference, and discussion | 5 |
| At least one figure shows 3+ variables effectively | 5 |
| Findings clearly stated and tied to question(s) | 7 |
| Limitations and "what we'd do next" discussion | 4 |
| Citations of data and sources | 4 |

## Code quality (15)

| Component | Points |
|---|---|
| Code runs cleanly (renders from scratch) | 5 |
| Follows a named style guide | 3 |
| Meaningfully commented | 3 |
| Code in appendix, not body | 2 |
| Reproducible from GitHub repo | 2 |

## Reproducibility (10)

| Component | Points |
|---|---|
| GitHub repo well-organized with README | 4 |
| All team members have commits | 3 |
| Data accessible (in repo or via working URL) | 3 |

## Video presentation (10)

| Component | Points |
|---|---|
| Within time limit (5–7 min) | 2 |
| Each team member speaks | 3 |
| Covers question, data, key findings | 3 |
| Coherent, well-paced, audible | 2 |

## AI use & integrity (5)

| Component | Points |
|---|---|
| AI use note included in report | 2 |
| Honest attribution of external sources | 3 |

**Total: 100**

# Individual adjustments

Project grades may be adjusted up or down by up to 10 points per individual based on the oral checkpoints and observable team contribution. A student who could not engage with the project at the oral checkpoints will receive a lower individual grade than their team's collective work would suggest.

# Late policy

Late work for the final project requires a request to the instructor **at least 24 hours before the deadline**, with evidence of progress and a proposed new deadline. Without prior arrangement, late final reports lose 10 points per 24 hours.
